name: The Residence Maldives
shortIntro: Tucked on the island of Falhumaafushi in Huvadhoo Atoll, this resort pairs traditional Maldivian architecture with a quieter, more contemporary style of luxury. The surrounding reef is home to over 1,200 species of fish and coral, and the resort takes part in a Ministry-endorsed seagrass conservation project to help protect it. Beachfront and overwater villas both put you steps from the water, making it an easy pick for couples and families alike.

name: Pullman Maldives Maamutaa
shortIntro: Set on Maamutaa Island in Huvadhoo Atoll, this all-inclusive resort spans 122 villas built around a natural lake, including two rare Aqua Villas with underwater bedrooms. Diving is a real strength here — the on-site PADI centre is one of only four certified Eco Centres in the whole of the Maldives. With unlimited dining and water sports built into the stay, it suits both couples chasing a splurge and families needing everything sorted in advance.

name: Maamendhoo Fishing Lodge
shortIntro: A privately-run lodge on Maamendhoo island, right in the heart of Huvadhoo Atoll, built specifically around sport fishing. Your stay includes all meals, soft drinks, and laundry, with guided trips out to nearby lagoons and reefs chasing Giant Trevally, Yellowfin Tuna, and more. The dry season from November to April brings the calmest, clearest water for fishing.

name: Huvadhoo Lodge & Dive
shortIntro: A PADI dive centre and lodge on Maamendhoo island, led by an instructor with over 15 years of experience across the Maldives. Huvadhoo Atoll has the country's largest shark population, highest number of turtles, and some of its healthiest coral reefs, making this a strong base for both first-time divers and experienced ones chasing bigger marine life.

name: Mercure Maldives Kooddoo Resort
shortIntro: An adults-only resort on Kooddoo island in Huvadhoo Atoll, with 70 villas ranging from beachfront to overwater. The house reef is rich with marine life, and nearby channel dives bring you close to larger pelagic species. Choose from all-inclusive, bed & breakfast, or half-board depending on how you like to eat on holiday.
